description: A postsecondary psychology teacher is preparing a 'Sleep, Fatigue, and Cognition' laboratory module using 10-minute PVT data from 16 participants (P00-P15) in rested and sleep-deprived sessions located at ~/pebl/data/pvt_session_data.csv. Analyze the data to identify a fabricated participant (defined by RT variability SD < 5ms) and compute performance metrics for all valid participants. For each valid participant and condition, calculate mean_rt_ms (excluding false starts < 100ms), median_rt_ms (excluding false starts), lapses (RT > 500ms), false_starts (RT < 100ms), fastest_10pct_ms, and slowest_10pct_ms. Additionally, determine the vigilance_slope_ms_per_quintile by dividing each session's 120 trials into five 24-trial quintiles and calculating the linear regression slope of mean RT against quintile number (1-5). Compute group-level means for mean_rt_ms, median_rt_ms, lapses, and vigilance_slope_ms_per_quintile for both conditions, along with the sleep_deprivation_effect_ms (deprived mean - rested mean). Save the results to ~/pebl/analysis/pvt_report.json including a 'participants' list with individual metrics or exclusion reasons, a 'group_means' object, and the 'sleep_deprivation_effect_ms' key.
